chapter_title.en: Chapter 110 – The guarantee of the loan and the entrustment

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/287)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن وآخره مرسل - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (9/287)
text: صَاحِبُ الْوَدِيعَةِ وَ الْبِضَاعَةِ مُؤْتَمَنَانِ وَ قَالَ إِذَا هَلَكَتِ الْعَارِيَّةُ عِنْدَ الْمُسْتَعِيرِ لَمْ يَضْمَنْهُ إِلَّا أَنْ يَكُونَ قَدِ اشْتُرِطَ عَلَيْهِ
وَ قَالَ فِي حَدِيثٍ آخَرَ إِذَا كَانَ مُسْلِماً عَدْلًا فَلَيْسَ عَلَيْهِ ضَمَانٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Hammad, from Al Halby,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘The owner of the entrustment and the merchandise are both entrusted’. And he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘When the borrowed item perishes with the borrower, he would not be responsible except if there happened to be a stipulation upon him’.
And he<sup>asws</sup> said in another Hadeeth: ‘When he was a just Muslim, so there is no liability upon him’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hammad from al-Halabiy who has said the following: “About the case of a man who claims that Wadi‘ah (safe deposit) were entrusted assets, abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, has said, ‘If a borrowed item is destroyed with the borrower he is not held liable unless it is stipulated.”’ In another Hadith it is said that if the borrower is a just Muslim he is not held liable.
